result:
Done — `python3 -m pytest -q` passes with all 6 tests green. There were two bugs, both in `lrucache/cache.py`:

1. **`get` never refreshed recency** (`cache.py:23`): a cache hit returned the value without calling `move_to_end`, so reading an entry didn't mark it as recently used and it could still be evicted first. Added `self._data.move_to_end(key)` on a successful, non-expired hit.

2. **`put` evicted on in-place updates** (`cache.py:33`): the eviction check only looked at `len >= capacity`, so overwriting an existing key in a full cache wrongly evicted an unrelated LRU entry. The eviction now only happens when the key is new.

Both fixes and their root causes are documented in `SOLUTION.md`. No test files were modified and no commits were made.
